title: interview intent
description:
Structured campaign intent produced by an interview between the hpc agent and either an external orchestrator or a human.

Deliberately bare-bones: captures *why* (goal, transcript,
provenance), *how big* (task_count), *what tags* (task_kind),
and *what limits* (budget, abort_if). Does NOT typed-encode the
search space — that would narrow the existing
experiment-agnostic tasks.py contract.

task_count.description: Expected number of tasks. The materializer asserts tasks.total() == task_count and refuses to write interview.json on mismatch — catches off-by-one bugs in the agent-written tasks.py at the interview stage.
task_count.title: Task Count

task_kind.description: Free-text tag the caller picks to group related campaigns for recall queries. hpc-agent does not own this vocabulary — there is no enum, no canonical set, and the framework treats it as an opaque string. Common shapes callers use today (purely as examples; you are not required to match them): 'ml-hparam-sweep', 'rl-rollout', 'llm-prompt-eval', 'benchmark-perf', 'data-shard'. recall groups by exact-match on whatever string the caller wrote, so sticking with a stable vocabulary within one project makes the rollup more useful — but that's a caller-side convention, not a framework requirement.
task_kind.title: Task Kind

budget.description: Soft caps. Opaque dict — units chosen by interview agent.

cluster_target.description: Optional. When present, submit_flow uses these directly. When omitted, the planner is invoked.

produced_by.description: Who/what produced this intent. Indexed by cmd_recall.

transcript.description: Interview Q/A turns. Optional but strongly recommended for human interviews — the value of being able to explain 'why did this campaign target cluster X' three months later vastly outweighs the few KB of storage. For agent-driven interviews this is typically the agent's tool-call trace.
transcript.title: Transcript

notes.description: Free-form supplementary notes from the interview.
notes.title: Notes

task_generator.description: Optional opt-in: when present, the materializer regenerates tasks.py from a typed recipe instead of consuming the agent-written one. The five shapes cover common ground without locking out exotic campaigns. Materializer cross-checks the produced task count against intent.task_count before any disk writes.
task_generator.title: Task Generator

entry_point.description: Optional declaration of the experiment's entry point. Three shapes: ``register_run`` (pointer to an existing @register_run notebook function — no materialization), ``python_module`` (importable Python module + function), ``shell_command`` (argv + signature; the interview materializes a ``@register_run`` wrapper at ``.hpc/wrappers/<run_name>.py`` that subprocess-invokes the argv). Lets a mature repo with main.py + frozen YAML configs participate in the same intake as a greenfield notebook — the wrapper gives the framework something to introspect (signature, classify-axis, validate-executor-signatures) while main.py stays untouched.
entry_point.title: Entry Point

axes.description: Named axes; tasks = full Cartesian product. resolve(i) returns a dict with one key per axis.
axes.title: Axes

items.description: List of task-kwargs dicts. Most agnostic shape — just stores the list verbatim and resolve(i) returns items[i].
items.title: Items

session_sha.description: Agent/orchestrator session identifier. Required when kind=agent; null otherwise.
session_sha.title: Session Sha

operator.description: Human operator name/handle when kind=human; null when kind=agent.
operator.title: Operator

_PythonModuleEntry.description:
Mature-repo entry point: an importable Python function (no notebook).

The framework can already introspect importable Python; this kind is
a wire hint so the interview can validate the module/function exists
before submit. No wrapper is materialized.

function.description: The function inside ``module`` to treat as the entry point.
function.title: Function

_PythonModuleEntry.title: _PythonModuleEntry

_RegisterRunEntry.description:
Notebook entry point: an existing ``@register_run``-decorated function.

Pure pointer — no materialization. The framework's existing
``discover_runs`` walks ``notebooks/`` to find the function by
``run_name``. Provided as a kind so the interview's entry-point
field is total over the shapes the framework supports, not just
the ones that need materialization.

run_name.description: The ``@register_run`` function name to discover in notebooks/.
run_name.title: Run Name

_RegisterRunEntry.title: _RegisterRunEntry

_ShellCommandEntry.description:
Mature-repo entry point: a shell command (``main.py``, compiled binary, ...).

The interview materializes a thin ``@register_run`` wrapper at
``.hpc/wrappers/<run_name>.py`` whose body subprocess-invokes the
argv with kwargs substituted in. The wrapper's *signature* (built
from ``signature``) is what downstream introspection reads;
``main.py`` stays opaque to the framework.

``frozen_configs`` lets the experimenter declare config files
whose content is part of the experiment's identity. The
materializer hashes each path's bytes; the interview threads
``<basename>_sha`` into every materialized task's kwargs so the
framework's ``cmd_sha`` correctly distinguishes ``exp_42.yaml``
from ``exp_43.yaml`` (and catches accidental in-place edits).

run_name.description: Name for the generated wrapper file (``.hpc/wrappers/<run_name>.py``) and the ``@register_run``-decorated function inside it. Must be a valid Python identifier.
run_name.title: Run Name

argv.description: Argv template. ``{param}`` placeholders are substituted from the wrapper's kwargs at call time. Example: ["python3", "main.py", "--config", "{config}", "--seed", "{seed}"].
argv.title: Argv

signature.description: Wrapper signature: ``{param_name: type_str}``. Each name must be a valid Python identifier; types are 'str' / 'int' / 'float' / 'bool'. The wrapper also takes ``**kwargs`` so framework-injected identity fields (e.g. ``config_sha``) flow through without polluting main.py.
signature.title: Signature

frozen_configs.description: Paths (relative to campaign_dir) to config files whose content should be part of the experiment's identity. For each entry the interview hashes the bytes and threads ``<basename>_sha`` into every task's kwargs.
frozen_configs.title: Frozen Configs

_ShellCommandEntry.title: _ShellCommandEntry
